issue_title: `awatch` on windows can't be killed with Ctrl+C
issue_text:
The "KeyboardInterrupt" gets logged but the process doesn't terminate until the next file system event happens and `RustNotify.watch()` returns.

This is because windows doesn't have signals, so `open_signal_receiver`

https://github.com/samuelcolvin/watchfiles/blob/7a74df9d1a403fcf57e9b27ac65cfb30c2dbf8c9/watchfiles/main.py#L181

Doesn't work and there's no way to tell the rust code to terminate.

Potential solution is to return from `RustNotify.watch()` regularly, and thereby check if
success_criteria: 应修复 issue《`awatch` on windows can't be killed with Ctrl+C》中描述的错误行为；核心逻辑预计位于 `watchfiles/main.py`；并补齐 1 到 3 个稳定回归测试，确保当前错误行为消失且相邻边界不回归。

metadata.ready_note: 已压成可运行 semi-real 回归任务；当前已纳入 challenge manifest，作为第 2 条 challenge hard case 持续观察。
